Integrated marketing · Lifecycle & growth

Marketing built like a system, run like a small team of one.

I'm Jean Wu, a marketing operator with 7+ years across the US and Greater China. At a $1B+ private-markets platform I run the full program (lifecycle email, webinars, events, SEO, WeChat) and build my own AI-assisted tools to run it faster, the kind of work most teams hand to engineering. Before fintech: my own integrated marketing studio and luxury brands like Tom Ford and Aesop.

Based in Los Angeles Works in EN / 中文

01BuildUpMarket · 2025–Present

I build my own marketing tools with AI

A two-person marketing team, so I built the tooling myself — directing AI end to end to ship production tools usually handed to engineers: a WeChat publisher and a multi-channel content tracker. Both are live below; click around.

WeChat publishing tool
On-brand EN / 中文 templates with live preview and one-click export — drafted, formatted and ready in minutes.
Hours → minutes per on-brand article
Multi-channel content tracker
Calendar and Kanban views planning a month across LinkedIn, X, WeChat and email, tracking every post through review.
One source of truth for a lean team
A real tool I built for work, with sample content for this demo. Try the templates and live preview. The production version connects to live content and AI for auto-formatting.
Plan a month of content across LinkedIn, X, WeChat and email around a webinar anchor. Switch between calendar and Kanban, filter by channel, track each post through the review pipeline. Sample data.

About

I'm always trying to understand how people actually live, not how they're supposed to.

I grew up moving between cultures, spent my twenties building a creative studio and a career in Shanghai, and now call Los Angeles home. Seven-plus years of marketing across two markets taught me how taste, language and timing shift from one audience to the next.

I notice how people move through a space, how a phrase lands differently in Mandarin than in English, how a good system quietly makes life easier. That instinct runs through the creative work and the operational work, and lately through the AI tools I build to close the gap between the two.

Beauty without purpose is just noise; a system without a story is just plumbing. I try to do both.

Currently reading
  • The Design of Everyday Things by Don Norman
  • Unreasonable Hospitality by Will Guidara
  • Siddhartha by Hermann Hesse
